tema1.question: Which is true?

answers.answer: In a single-processor environment, concurrent processes may overlap their instructions.
In a multi-processor environment, concurrent processes may interleave their instructions.
In a multi-processor environment, concurrent processes always overlap their instructions.
None of the above is true

tema1.question: "Some tasks must finish before others start" refers to... (choose best)

answers.answer: Critical section.
Serialization.
Mutual Exclusion.
Concurrency.

tema1.question: "The scheduler is fair" means

answers.answer: All processes receive the same amount of cpu-time.
All processes having the same priority receive the same amount of cpu-time.
There is an upper bound to the time a process is "ready" but not "running"
None of the above

tema1.question: Consider... A1: in a multi-processor environment, non-determinism can cause a sequential program to yield different results in different executions that take the same input data. A2: in a single-processor environment, non-determinism can cause a concurrent program to yield different results in different executions that take the same input data. A3: in a multi-processor environment, non-determinism can result in a concurrent program showing interference in some executions and not showing it in some others even when all executions take the same input data.

answers.answer: A1, A2 and A3 are true
Only A3 is true
Only A2 and A3 are true
All of them are false

tema1.question: The concurrent update problem is an example of

answers.answer: Mutual exclusion
Serialization
Interference
Starvation

tema1.question: th1 and th2 are Java threads...

answers.answer: th1 can stop th2 invoking th2.stop().
th1 can put th2 to sleep invoking th2.sleep(time).
th1 can suggest th2 to yield the processor invoking th2.yield().
None of the above is true

tema1.question: Signalling a general semaphore when no process is waiting on it...

answers.answer: Never has an effect (= nothing happens)
Always has an effect and never generates an error
Always generates an error
Sometimes has an effect and sometimes has no effect at all.

tema1.question: A lock...

answers.answer: Can only be acquired by the owner but can be released by any process
Can be acquired and released by any process
Can be acquired by any process but can only be released by the owner
None of the above

tema1.question: Pooling...

answers.answer: May increase efficiency by avoiding frequent creation and destruction of threads
May increase efficiency by managing shared variables and/or message passing
Avoids interference by enforcing synchronization policies
None of the above

tema1.question: Mutual exclusion is an instance of... (choose best)

answers.answer: Indeterminism
Interference
Communication
Synchronization

tema1.question: "Once a process has requested to enter the critical section, there's a maximum amount of time it will have to wait until the request is granted "

answers.answer: This is a correctness property of the critical section problem known as "bounded waiting"
This is a correctness property of the critical section problem known as "progress property"
This is a correctness property of the critical section problem known as "freedom from starvation"
None of the above

tema1.question: Regarding the critical section problem, which assumption is true

answers.answer: A process may stay forever in its non-critical section
A variable used in the protocols can also be used in the non-critical section
The scheduler gives the same number of opportunities to each process
A process may crash while executing the post-protocol

tema1.question: Regarding test & set... A1: The test & set variable has an owner and only this owner can change its value A2: The test and set variable has an owner and only the owner can check its value

answers.answer: Both A1 and A2 are true
Both A1 and A2 are false
A1 is true but A2 is false
A1 is false but A2 is true

tema1.question: In concurrent programs, the absence of starvation...

answers.answer: Is a safety property
Is a liveness property
Is a mutual exclusions property
None of the above

tema1.question: Which of the following is not a necessary condition for deadlock

answers.answer: Incremental acquisition
Mutual exclusion
Freedom from interference
None of the above (all three are necessary conditions for deadlock)

tema1.question: An asynchronous channel

answers.answer: Can block the receiver but may never block the sender
Can block the sender but may never block the receiver
Can block both the sender and the receiver
Can neither block the sender nor the receiver

tema1.question: Java sockets are an implementation of

answers.answer: One-way synchronous channels
One-way asynchronous channels
Two-way synchronous channels
Two-way asynchronous channels

tema1.question: How many of the following statements are true A1: In multi-processor environments, concurrent processes always overlap their instructions. A2: in single-processor environments, concurrent processes may under certain circumstances overlap their instructions. A3: in single-processor environments, concurrent programs behave deterministically.
"Certain parts of the code cannot overlap their instructions" refers to... (choose best, if any)

answers.answer: Deadlock avoidance
Serialization
Mutual Exclusion.
None of the above

tema1.question: How many of the following are assumptions in the Critical Section Problem: The scheduler follows a policy that gives each process an opportunity (at least) to be executed. Any process will request to enter its critical section at least once. Any process executing its critical section will exit it before other processes request to enter their critical sections. At any one time, only one process is executing its non-critical section
An interleaving of instructions that leads to an incorrect update of a shared variable is a

answers.answer: Deadlock
Race condition
Critical Section
None of the above

tema1.question: How many of the following invocations ALWAYS block the invoker: Invoking wait (acquire in Java) on a semaphore. Invoking waitC (await in Java) on a monitor condition. Invoking lock on a lock. Invoking compareAndSet in an AtomicInteger

answers.answer: More than 2

tema1.question: How many of the following operations may have no effect whatsoever: Invoking signal (acquire in Java) on a general semaphore. Invoking signalC (signal in Java) on a monitor condition. Invoking unlock on a lock
C/S is an asynchronous distributed pattern. Here, asynchronous means...

answers.answer: Receive operations never block the receiver. Send operations may block the sender
Send operations never block the sender. Receive operations may block the receiver
Both receive and send may block the invoker.
None of the above

tema1.question: Which of the following assertions is/are true? A1: in a single-processor environment, concurrent processes may overlap their instructions. A2: in a multi-processor environment, concurrent processes may interleave their instructions. A3: in a multi-processor environment, concurrent processes always overlap their instructions.

answers.answer: All of them.
A1 and A2
A2 and A3
Only A2

tema1.question: Which of the following assertions is/are true? A1: the concurrent update problem is an example of starvation. A2: for a scheduler to be fair it must prioritize processes that have been waiting the longest time. A3: Mutual Exclusion is a type of serialization.

answers.answer: The three of them
None
A1 and A3
Only A3

tema1.question: Which is true:

answers.answer: In a single-processor environment, non-determinism can cause a concurrent program to yield different results in different executions that take the same input data.
Non-determinism causes correct programs yield incorrect results.
Non-determinism only happens in multiple-processor environments.
None

tema1.question: In java, how many of the following methods defined in class Thread are instance methods sleep(), yield(), join()
Which is true of a MESA-Style monitor

answers.answer: When a process invokes the signalC operation on a condition, it immediately releases the lock
When a process invokes the waitC operation on a condition, it is not blocked if the condition is true
When a process invokes the waitC operation on a condition, it immediately releases the lock
None of the above

tema1.question: Which is true regarding the channel-based client/server pattern

answers.answer: It's symmetrical with regard to naming and synchronous with regard to message passing.
It's asymmetrical with regard to naming and synchronous with regard to message passing.
It's symmetrical with regard to naming and asynchronous with regard to message passing.
It's asymmetrical with regard to naming and asynchronous with regard to message passing.

tema1.question: In Java RMI

answers.answer: Both the stub and the skeleton must implement the same interface (extending from Remote)
Both the stub and the server object must implement the same interface (extending from Remote)
Both the client object and the skeleton must implement the same interface (extending from remote)
None of the above

tema1.question: In Java RMI, which of the four components (client, server, skeleton and stub) are responsible of performing marshalling and unmarshalling operations?

answers.answer: Only the client and the server.
Only the server and the stub.
Only the skeleton and the client.
None of the above
